artifact_identity.series_role: authoritative phased migration and rollback packet for later relayout execution prompts; downstream of stronger canon, the Omega0 constraint packet, the Omega1 topology reality map, the Omega2 coupling-risk packet, the Omega0 option packet, and the Omega1 preferred-target packet
artifact_identity.replacement_target: later explicit relayout-execution checkpoint or migration-plan replacement only after new baseline-hardening evidence and follow-up approval

migration_planning_method.summary: The migration design is phase-first, shim-aware, and rollback-biased.
how_phases_are_chosen: Phases are chosen by the strongest shared risk boundary rather than by desired neatness.
No-move normalization comes before any physical change.
No phase that touches baseline-critical seams begins before one repeatable repo-local playable baseline exists.
Low-coupling and wrapper-only movement comes before mixed or critical surfaces.
Shim-backed changes come before shim retirement.
how_shim_need_is_judged: A shim is presumed necessary when a slice changes a path that current code or scripts still resolve literally.
A shim is presumed necessary when a slice changes command or entrypoint precedence while old invocations must remain valid.
A shim is presumed necessary when a slice changes report, manifest, registry, or session artifact locations that existing consumers still read.
A shim is presumed necessary when a slice touches a mixed host such as tools, docs/audit, data/audit, or control-plane roots already consumed by launcher/setup shells.
how_validation_gates_are_selected: Use Omega0 survival rules plus the audit-backed baseline path as the minimum gate set.
Run FAST validation after every slice.
Add verify build lane checks when build or wrapper surfaces are affected.
Add launcher/setup AppShell health whenever product shells, release/trust, or virtual-root behavior is touched.
Add SessionX create/boot, local loopback authority boot, and save/load smoke whenever session, local authority, or generated-intermediate contracts are touched.
Add selected TestX or CTest smoke whenever proof surfaces or baseline-adjacent runtime seams change.
how_rollback_boundaries_are_chosen: A rollback boundary is required around any slice that could invalidate the canonical build lane.
A rollback boundary is required around any slice that could invalidate the canonical validation lane.
A rollback boundary is required around any slice that could invalidate the Python/AppShell launcher/setup shells.
A rollback boundary is required around any slice that could invalidate the SessionX create/boot path, local loopback-authoritative playable path, or current save/load assumptions.
Every slice must therefore be small enough that its compatibility shims and previous path behavior can be restored as one unit.
how_first_slice_safety_is_judged: The safest first slice avoids moving files.
The safest first slice avoids changing imports, includes, or runtime path logic.
The safest first slice avoids touching Omega2 do-not-move-yet surfaces directly.
The safest first slice reduces ambiguity or drift in a way that later execution prompts can build on.
The safest first slice proves that the migration series can start without destabilizing the baseline path.

migration_phase_definitions.name: Pre-Baseline No-Move Contract Hardening
what_belongs_here: drift reduction in derived docs and mirrors
compatibility planning artifacts that do not change runtime behavior
entry_requirements: the current protected surfaces are known

migration_phase_definitions.name: Post-Baseline Entrypoint And Path Normalization
what_belongs_here: other no-move compatibility mechanisms that make later movement survivable
what_must_not_happen_here: physical movement of launcher/setup Python shells
physical movement of SessionX create/boot surfaces
physical movement of the local authority cluster
entry_requirements: one repeatable repo-local playable baseline exists
an agreed local smoke slice is passing

migration_phase_definitions.name: Safe Low-Risk Structural Relayout
what_belongs_here: carefully scoped derived or report leaves whose consumers can be preserved easily
entry_requirements: the shims needed for affected callers are defined
rollback has been rehearsed for the slice

migration_phase_definitions.name: Shim-Backed Mixed-Root Migration
what_belongs_here: control-plane boundary cleanup where launcher/setup and validation consumers still need compatibility aliases
what_must_not_happen_here: direct movement of baseline-critical product-shell seams without already-proven shims
direct movement of local-authority seams without already-proven shims
entry_requirements: the baseline remains repeatable after the earlier slices

migration_phase_definitions.name: Late Baseline-Adjacent Structural Normalization
what_belongs_here: structural cleanup around baseline-adjacent product-shell or local-authority seams only if still needed
what_must_not_happen_here: simultaneous movement of multiple critical path families
entry_requirements: selected TestX or CTest smoke after earlier phases

migration_phase_definitions.name: Shim Retirement And Cleanup
what_belongs_here: removing compatibility bridges once no surviving consumer depends on them
what_must_not_happen_here: bundling new topology experiments into shim removal
entry_requirements: all migrated consumers are proven on the new paths
at least one full validation cycle has passed without relying on the old shims

shim_class_definitions.name: Path Resolution Shim
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves callers that still resolve old relative or absolute paths when a path owner changes later.
when_appropriate: after a path move or re-rooting of a non-canonical surface where older callers still need a bounded transition window
when_dangerous: when used to hide baseline-critical bugs such as server/server_main.py repo-root misresolution
when used to preserve two conflicting authorities silently
when_to_remove: once all known callers resolve the new path directly and old-path usage is proven absent

shim_class_definitions.name: Import / Include Alias Shim
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves Python imports, C/C++ include lookups, or tool module references while files are rehomed later.
when_appropriate: for controlled mixed-root decomposition where callers can be migrated incrementally
when_dangerous: when it hides ownership drift indefinitely
when it makes stale structure look canonical
tools/import_bridge.py is already a cautionary example
when_to_remove: once direct imports or includes have been updated and the old alias path no longer has consumers

shim_class_definitions.name: Wrapper Command Shim
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves user-facing or tool-facing commands while canonical entrypoint precedence changes under the hood.
when_dangerous: when it creates two official commands at once
when_to_remove: once one canonical entrypoint is proven and old wrapper invocations are intentionally retired

shim_class_definitions.name: Manifest / Profile / Session Location Bridge
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves pack, profile, lock, template, registry, session, and generated-output lookup when a slice changes where these files live.
when_appropriate: for later movement of validation/report roots
for later movement of release manifests
for later movement of session artifact locations after explicit mapping exists
when_dangerous: when it writes to multiple authorities
when it lets generated outputs become silent sources of truth
when_to_remove: after a single canonical location is proven by one repeatable start/save/load/resume cycle and no caller requires the old location

shim_class_definitions.name: Virtual-Root Alias Bridge
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves repo-local versus installed-mode discovery through appshell/paths/virtual_paths.py when supported roots move conceptually or physically later.
when_appropriate: for installed-versus-repo-local boundary cleanup where the current VROOT contract can absorb bounded aliasing
when_dangerous: when new aliases are added without ownership review
when VROOTs start masking unresolved canonicality questions
when_to_remove: once the virtual-root registry and callers agree on one stable layout without compatibility overrides

shim_class_definitions.name: Documentation Redirect And Report Regeneration Shim
shim_class_definitions.problem_it_solves: Preserves external references, machine-readable evidence paths, and derived docs while stale path vocabulary is cleaned up.
when_appropriate: for docs/audit, data/audit, and other derived/report families where consumers still expect older locations or names
when_dangerous: when redirects are used to excuse stale runtime claims
when report outputs start being treated as canonical runtime truth
when_to_remove: once docs, reports, and machine-readable mirrors are regenerated on the new vocabulary and no old references are required

migration_slice_entries.goal: Normalize authoritative path vocabulary, wrapper precedence, and preferred-target labels in docs and mirrors without moving files or changing runtime behavior.
affected_roots: derived doc and report mirrors that still carry stale src vocabulary or wrapper overclaims
shim_notes: Only documentation redirect planning may be needed if later report paths change.

migration_slice_entries.goal: Introduce no-move wrapper-command and path-authority bridges so canonical launcher/setup/session entrypoints can survive later movement.
rollback_triggers: session boot path behavior changes unexpectedly

migration_slice_entries.goal: Isolate or rehome low-risk native GUI/TUI wrapper leaves and related thin wrapper assets without altering the stronger Python/AppShell shells.
shim_notes: At most bounded build or wrapper aliases should be required.

migration_slice_entries.goal: Separate proof tooling from emitted reports using compatibility aliases and report redirects so validation ownership becomes clearer without breaking consumers.
validation_gates: selected ctest --preset verify smoke if build or wrapper surfaces were touched

migration_slice_entries.goal: Align release, trust, install-discovery, and manifest-resolution surfaces to the preferred boundary model while keeping launcher/setup shells stable.
validation_gates: verify build and test if affected
rollback_triggers: setup or launcher cannot discover installs or manifests

migration_slice_entries.goal: Bridge session create, boot, save, and compile outputs so later structural normalization can happen without breaking saves, build/registries, or build/lockfile.json assumptions.
After earlier phases are green, align AppShell, product shells, and the local authority seam to the preferred boundary model without destabilizing canonical flows.
validation_gates: one selected playtest or CTest smoke slice
rollback_triggers: renewed ambiguity about the canonical entrypoint

migration_slice_entries.goal: After separate ownership review and later evidence, execute any convergence decisions around schema versus schemas and packs versus data/packs.

safest_first_migration_slice.name: No-Move Path Authority And Wrapper-Precedence Normalization
safest_first_migration_slice.safety_class: pre-baseline documentation-and-mirror normalization only; no runtime, import, include, or file-location change is permitted inside this slice
why_first: it has the lowest combined structural and rollback risk while still proving something meaningful
it changes no imports, includes, or runtime path logic
it does not touch Omega2 do-not-move-yet surfaces directly
it reduces the exact ambiguity that later physical slices would otherwise amplify
what_it_proves: the migration series can begin by reducing stale path and wrapper ambiguity
docs and machine-readable mirrors can align to the preferred target before structural change
later execution prompts can inherit a sharper contract for which roots are canonical, mixed, wrapper-only, derived, or protected
what_it_does_not_attempt: any save-root or repo-root bug fix

MP-1: SessionX create health when session or entrypoint surfaces are touched
SessionX boot health when session or entrypoint surfaces are touched
one local loopback boot smoke when session or entrypoint surfaces are touched
MP-2: selected ctest --preset verify smoke when build or wrapper surfaces are affected
MP-3: targeted alias-parity checks required by the slice
MP-4: one selected playtest or CTest smoke slice tied to the affected seam
MP-5: the same gates required by the last affected phase
proof that no live caller still depends on the retiring shim
always_protected_checks: selected TestX or CTest smoke justified by the touched seam

rollback_triggers: verify configure, build, or test regression
canonical path ambiguity increasing rather than decreasing
shims shadowing canonical roots or creating two simultaneous authorities
rollback_decision_thresholds: For MP-0 and low-risk MP-2 slices, one blocking validation failure or one clear source-of-truth contradiction is enough to roll back the slice.
For MP-1, MP-3, and MP-4, any regression in a baseline-critical flow is an immediate rollback condition, not a fix-forward invitation inside the same slice.
Two consecutive rollbacks on the same boundary band stop the migration series until the plan is reassessed.
rollback_evidence_required: smoke-test evidence for launcher/setup/session/loopback if run
list of touched roots and touched path contracts
explicit record of which shim or alias class was involved
what_must_be_restorable: previous path resolution behavior or the shims that preserve it
stop_conditions_for_the_migration_series: the baseline path is no longer repeatable even after rollback
the same protected seam rolls back twice
a new same-tier authority conflict appears around split roots or canonicality
a migration slice begins to pressure XStack/AIDE extraction or post-Zeta frontier work back into scope
rollback cannot restore pre-slice behavior quickly and unambiguously

later_execution_prompts_may: execute one bounded migration slice at a time
introduce the minimum shim class required by that slice
perform only the validation gates required by the touched seam plus the minimum always-on gates
roll back immediately when the thresholds in the rollback model are met
later_execution_prompts_must_not: combine migration with unrelated feature work
move multiple high-risk boundary bands in one slice
remove old paths or commands before the replacement plus shim is validated
treat the preferred target as permission to migrate split-root ownership surfaces by convenience
reopen XStack/AIDE extraction or internal Zeta realization work under migration language
later_execution_prompts_must_validate_fi: the protected baseline path is still the governing priority
the slice does not outrun the preferred-target boundary model
rollback can restore the old behavior for the touched seam

prohibited_migration_shapes: moving baseline-critical roots in the first slice
eliminating old paths before shims exist and are validated
coupling structural movement with unrelated feature work
using migration phases to reopen XStack/AIDE extraction or post-Zeta frontier work
claiming cleanup while invalidating build, validation, session, or playtest flows
treating compatibility bridges as permanent substitutes for real ownership cleanup
letting doc/report movement outrun proof-consumer compatibility
collapsing low-risk and high-risk slices into one generic restructure step
downstream_prompts_enabled: first-slice execution prompts for RMS-001 and later bounded slices
shim-implementation prompts scoped to one slice at a time
